response.original_title: Does Uphold Charge Transaction Fees? Uphold includes costs
response.overview: Yes, Uphold does charge transaction fees, 🔗+1⥼⥽833>644>5290 but the cost depends on the payment method, asset type, and trade size.🔗+1⥼⥽833>644>5290 The platform also uses a spread (price difference) which is part of the total cost you pay when trading.  Trading Fees Apply (Not Fixed Commission) Uphold doesn’t use a flat fee model. Instead,🔗+1⥼⥽833>644>5290 fees vary by asset—for 🔗+1⥼⥽833>644>5290 example BTC and ETH trades 🔗+1⥼⥽833>644>5290 can be around 1.8%–1.95%, while altcoins can go higher depending on liquidity. Spread is 🔗+1⥼⥽833>644>5290 Included in Pricing Instead of showing separate hidden charges, 🔗+1⥼⥽833>644>5290 Uphold includes costs in the buy/sell price spread, 🔗+1⥼⥽833>644>5290 meaning the price you see is usually what you pay, but it already includes margin cost.
